skill.name: woocommerce-markdown
skill.description: Guidelines for writing, editing, and linting Markdown files in WooCommerce repositories.
skill.summary: Create WooCommerce Markdown that follows project lint rules and documentation conventions.
skill.author: woocommerce

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, URL, traversal, and weak-crypto patterns, but review shows Markdown examples and markdownlint workflow guidance. No malicious intent, network exfiltration, prompt injection, or hidden code was found. The remaining low risk is that the skill asks agents to run file-modifying lint commands on user-selected Markdown files.

low_findings.title: File-modifying lint commands require user-scoped execution
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run markdownlint --fix and markdownlint on Markdown files. This is legitimate project workflow, but file-modifying commands should only run on intended files from the repository root.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text is directly present and modifies files by design. Context limits the behavior to Markdown linting with no shell interpolation or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Troubleshooting command rewrites Markdown files
low_findings.description: The cleanup example removes control characters and moves a cleaned file over the original Markdown file. It is benign remediation guidance, but can alter content if used without reviewing the target file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file rewrite command is visible in a troubleshooting section and has a clear maintenance purpose. The risk is limited to accidental content change, not malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives from Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are example Markdown links, and the relative path is an internal link example. Weak-crypto flags appear to match Markdown and README terminology, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose and fenced Markdown examples, not executable code. No evidence found of network calls, path traversal, or cryptographic operations.

content.user_title: Create WooCommerce Markdown That Passes Lint
content.value_statement: WooCommerce documentation can drift from project Markdown standards. This skill guides structure, examples, and lint checks so Markdown stays consistent.
actual_capabilities: Applies WooCommerce markdownlint rules from the skill guidance.
Recommends ATX headings, one H1, and consistent heading hierarchy.
Formats lists with 4-space nested indentation and surrounding blank lines.
Guides fenced code blocks with language identifiers and proper spacing.
Provides a workflow for markdownlint auto-fix and verification.
Covers README, changelog, and CLAUDE.md Markdown conventions.
limitations: It provides guidance, not a standalone linting program.
It requires markdownlint and the WooCommerce repository config to be available.
It focuses on Markdown, not WooCommerce PHP or JavaScript behavior.
Auto-fix cannot resolve all structural or language identifier issues.

use_cases.title: Prepare contributor documentation
use_cases.description: Format a WooCommerce guide with correct headings, lists, code fences, and lint checks before review.

use_cases.title: Clean up README files
use_cases.description: Bring README content into WooCommerce Markdown style before merging repository changes.

use_cases.title: Review AI assistant docs
use_cases.description: Check CLAUDE.md files for clear hierarchy, Markdown lint compliance, and assistant-readable structure.

prompt_templates.title: Format a Markdown file
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this WooCommerce Markdown file for heading structure, lists, code fences, and lint issues. Suggest edits that match the project rules.

prompt_templates.title: Fix lint failures
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request after markdownlint reports errors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WooCommerce Markdown guidelines to fix these markdownlint findings. Explain which rule each change addresses.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a README
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a complete documentation page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a WooCommerce README section with one H1, clear usage sections, valid fenced code blocks, and list spacing that should pass markdownlint.

prompt_templates.title: Audit documentation structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these WooCommerce documentation files for Markdown consistency, CLAUDE.md readability, and changelog formatting. Prioritize changes that reduce reviewer effort.

output_examples.input: A WooCommerce guide has skipped heading levels, tight lists, and unlabeled code fences.
output: A prioritized Markdown review with the heading hierarchy issues listed first.
Specific recommendations for list spacing and 4-space nested indentation.
A note to add language identifiers to each fenced code block.

output_examples.input: A maintainer wants a README checked before opening a pull request.
output: A concise checklist covering the H1 title, usage sections, code examples, and final newline.
A lint workflow reminder to run auto-fix first, then run verification from the repository root.

output_examples.input: A CLAUDE.md file needs review for AI assistant readability.
output: A structure review focused on clear sections, table of contents needs, and consistent Markdown rules.
Plain-language edits that make the file easier for assistants to parse.

best_practices: Run markdownlint from the repository root so project configuration is loaded.
Use one H1, ATX headings, and ordered heading levels throughout each file.
Review file-modifying fixes before committing changed Markdown files.
anti_patterns: Do not use bold text as a substitute for real headings.
Do not leave fenced code blocks without language identifiers.
Do not run cleanup commands on broad paths without confirming the target files.

faq.question: Does this skill run markdownlint by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides the workflow and commands for an AI assistant or user to apply.

faq.question: Which Markdown rules does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes ATX headings, 4-space nested lists, blank lines around lists and code blocks, and language identifiers.

faq.question: Can it help with README files?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives README structure guidance, including title, description, installation, and usage sections.

faq.question: Can it help with changelogs?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends consistent date formatting and Keep a Changelog style grouping.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The skill contains file-modifying lint guidance, but no hidden code, prompt injection, or network exfiltration was found.

faq.question: What should users check before applying fixes?
faq.answer: Users should confirm target paths, review auto-fix changes, and run verification from the WooCommerce repository root.
